network_events.title: Network Events
network_events.description: Browse recent connection, signal, and configuration events.
network_events.card_description: Recent network events captured by the device poller.
network_events.auto_refresh_paused: Auto-refresh paused — displaying events as of {{time}}.

email_alerts.page_description: Configure Gmail-based email notifications for connectivity outages.
email_alerts.card_description: Sends via Gmail SMTP using an app password.
email_alerts.not_installed_title: msmtp is not installed on this device.
email_alerts.not_installed_helper: Install automatically or run the command manually.
email_alerts.sender_email_description: The Gmail account that will send the alert.
email_alerts.app_password_description: Generate an <link>App Password</link> in your Google Account.
email_alerts.threshold_description: How long the connection must be down before an alert is sent. Prevents alerts for brief, transient outages.
email_alerts.save_before_test_hint: Save your changes before sending a test email.
email_alerts.uninstall_section_description: Uninstall the msmtp package from this device.
email_alerts.uninstall_confirm_description: This will remove the msmtp package from this device. Your email alert settings will be preserved, but alerts will not be sent until msmtp is reinstalled.
email_alerts.toast_save_error: Failed to save email alert settings
email_alerts.toast_test_error: Failed to send test email — check your configuration
email_alerts.log_card_description: History of sent and failed email alerts.
email_alerts.log_empty_hint_1: Alerts appear here when your connection drops past the configured threshold.
email_alerts.log_empty_hint_2: Use Send Test Email to verify your setup.

sms_alerts.page_description: Send SMS notifications via the device's cellular radio on connectivity outages.
sms_alerts.card_description: Sends SMS via your modem's cellular network.
sms_alerts.recipient_phone_description: Include the country code with a leading +, e.g. +14155551234.
sms_alerts.threshold_description: How long the connection must be down before an alert is sent.
sms_alerts.save_before_test_hint: Save your changes before sending a test SMS.
sms_alerts.toast_save_error: Failed to save SMS alert settings
sms_alerts.toast_test_error: Failed to send test SMS — check your configuration
sms_alerts.log_card_description: History of sent and failed SMS alerts.
sms_alerts.log_empty_hint_1: Alerts appear here when your connection drops past the configured threshold.
sms_alerts.log_empty_hint_2: Use Send Test SMS to verify your setup.

watchdog.page_description: Automatically detect and recover from internet outages with escalating recovery steps.
watchdog.settings_description: Configure connection health monitoring and recovery.
watchdog.auto_disabled_alert: Watchdog disabled itself after too many reboots in one hour. Re-enable it below once your connection is stable.
watchdog.failure_threshold_description: How many failed connectivity checks in a row before recovery begins.
watchdog.check_interval_description: How often the watchdog checks your internet connection.
watchdog.cooldown_description: Wait time after each recovery step before checking connectivity again.
watchdog.max_reboots_description: How many device reboots are allowed per hour before the watchdog locks itself.
watchdog.tier_3_enable_label: Enable Tier 3 — Switch to Backup SIM
watchdog.tier_1_description: Asks the modem to reconnect to your carrier's network. The gentlest first step.
watchdog.tier_2_description: Turns the modem's radio off and back on to force a fresh connection to the tower.
watchdog.tier_3_description: Switches to your backup SIM when the primary SIM can't get back online.
watchdog.tier_4_description: Restarts the whole device. Used only as a last resort when nothing else works.
watchdog.backup_sim_description: Which SIM slot to fail over to when Tier 3 activates.
watchdog.backup_sim_required_error: Choose a backup SIM slot or disable Tier 3.
watchdog.recovery_steps_description: Tried in order, from gentlest to most disruptive.
watchdog.tier_2_tooltip: Automatically skipped when tower lock is active to preserve your locked cells.
watchdog.toast_sim_revert_success: SIM revert requested. The watchdog will process this shortly.
watchdog.status_empty: Watchdog is not active. Enable it in Settings to begin monitoring connection health.
watchdog.status_starting: Watchdog is starting up. It will begin monitoring shortly.
watchdog.status_sim_failover_message: Running on backup SIM (slot {{current_slot}}) since {{switched_at}}. Original SIM was in slot {{original_slot}}.
watchdog.status_sim_revert_dialog_description: This will switch back to SIM slot {{original_slot}}. Your internet will briefly disconnect while the modem reconnects.
watchdog.quality_description: Trigger recovery when latency or packet loss stays over your limits — even while the connection is technically still up.
watchdog.quality_more_info_aria: More information about connection quality monitoring
watchdog.quality_tooltip: Latency and loss are read from the connection monitor as rolling averages. A breach must persist for the set number of consecutive checks before recovery runs.
watchdog.latency_ceiling_description: Average latency above this triggers recovery. Set 0 to ignore latency.
watchdog.latency_ceiling_error: Must be a number between 0 and 10000.
watchdog.loss_ceiling_description: Packet loss at or above this triggers recovery. Set 0 to ignore loss.
watchdog.loss_ceiling_error: Must be a number between 0 and 100.
watchdog.quality_consecutive_description: How many checks in a row must breach a ceiling before recovery runs.
watchdog.quality_consecutive_error: Must be a number between 1 and 60.
watchdog.quality_no_ceiling_error: Set at least one ceiling above 0, or recovery can never trigger.
watchdog.overview_description: Live connection health and the recovery actions taken.
watchdog.quality_off_hint: Off — recovery triggers on reachability only.
watchdog.state_off_subtitle: Turn it on to monitor connection health and auto-recover.
watchdog.state_blurb_suspect: A check just failed. Confirming before acting.
watchdog.state_blurb_recovery: Running a recovery step to restore the connection.
watchdog.state_blurb_cooldown: Waiting to see if the last step worked.
watchdog.triggers_description: When the watchdog decides the connection needs recovering.

latency.page_description: Monitor and analyze latency and packet loss to identify potential issues and optimize performance.
latency.card_description: Realtime ping results plus hourly, 12-hour, and daily averages.
latency.view_info_realtime: Real-time ping results from the last 50 seconds. Each bar represents a single ping.
latency.view_info_hourly: Hourly averages of latency and packet loss over the last 24 hours.
latency.view_info_12hour: 12-hour period averages of latency and packet loss.
latency.view_info_daily: Daily averages of latency and packet loss.
latency.entries_description: Raw ping measurements for the selected timeframe.
latency.entries_empty: No ping entries available for this timeframe.

tailscale.page_description: Manage your Tailscale mesh VPN connection and network peers.
tailscale.mutex_description: Only one VPN can be installed at a time. Uninstall {{other_vpn}} from the <link>NetBird page</link> first.
tailscale.not_installed_description: Install Tailscale to enable mesh VPN on this device.
tailscale.variant_official_description: Latest version, direct from Tailscale · ~50 MB on disk
tailscale.variant_tiny_description: Lightweight community package · smaller, older version
tailscale.service_stopped_description: The Tailscale service stopped or failed to start.
tailscale.needs_login_description: Open the login URL on a trusted device to authenticate.
tailscale.connected_description: Your device is connected to the Tailscale network.
tailscale.exit_node_description: Lets other devices route their internet traffic through this device — you must approve it in the Tailscale admin console.
tailscale.uninstall_confirm_description: This will remove Tailscale from this device and disconnect all peers.
tailscale.peers_empty_description: Devices will appear here as they connect to your Tailscale network.
tailscale.peers_not_connected_message: Connect to Tailscale to see your network peers.
tailscale.peers_no_peers_message: No peers found on your Tailscale network.
tailscale.logout_confirm_description: This will remove this device from your Tailscale network. You will need to re-authenticate to reconnect.
tailscale.remove_description: Uninstall the Tailscale packages and firewall rules from this device.
tailscale.reboot_required_description: Tailscale has been removed. A reboot is recommended to clean up firewall rules and other artifacts. Would you like to reboot now?

netbird.page_description: Manage your NetBird mesh VPN connection and network peers.
netbird.mutex_description: Only one VPN can be installed at a time. Uninstall {{other_vpn}} from the <link>Tailscale page</link> first.
netbird.not_installed_description: Install NetBird to enable mesh VPN on this device.
netbird.service_stopped_description: The NetBird service stopped or failed to start.
netbird.setup_key_description: Enter a setup key from your NetBird dashboard, or leave empty if already registered.
netbird.needs_login_description: Open the login URL on a trusted device to authenticate.
netbird.connected_description: Your device is connected to the NetBird network.
netbird.uninstall_confirm_description: This will remove the NetBird package, firewall rules, and all connection state from this device. The device will reboot to clean up any remaining artifacts.
netbird.peers_empty_description: Devices will appear here as they connect to your NetBird network.
netbird.peers_not_connected_message: Connect to NetBird to see your network peers.
netbird.peers_no_peers_message: No peers found on your NetBird network.
netbird.peers_no_detail_hint: Per-peer details are not available in this NetBird version. Upgrade to a newer version for detailed peer information.
netbird.remove_description: Uninstall the NetBird package and firewall rules from this device.
netbird.reboot_required_description: NetBird has been removed. A reboot is recommended to clean up firewall rules and other artifacts. Would you like to reboot now?
